steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: easy-tdx is an open-source Python library for accessing 通达信 (TongDaXin) market data over TCP. It includes a Python library at src/easy_tdx/, a Vue3 + TypeScript web UI in web-ui/, a FastAPI server, a Click CLI, 18 built-in trading strategies, and a backtesting engine. Start with the README to understand the project's purpose.

steps.title: CLI Entry Point
steps.description: The package is wired up via the easy_tdx.cli:cli Click group (pyproject.toml entry point). Each cmd_*.py file is one CLI subcommand — 25+ subcommands cover market info, quotes, kline, tick, transaction, backtest, chanlun, optimize, and admin.

steps.title: Protocol Codec Layer
steps.description: The lowest layer — pure binary encoders/decoders for the TDX TCP protocol. The 16-byte response frame (magic=0x0074CBB1), zlib body inflation, the variable-length signed price encoding, the 4-byte custom volume float, the MAC protocol 10-byte request frame, the financial-data .dat binary format, the industry-config .cfg parser, and the A-share price-limit rules engine. No I/O lives here.

steps.title: Protocol Command Layer
steps.description: One BaseCommand subclass per TDX protocol command. Each class encapsulates build_request (bytes out) and parse_response (dict/list in). Pure data shape — transport layer feeds them bytes. ~20 commands cover A-share quotes, K-line, transaction, finance, fund-flow, xdxr, company-info, minute data, and report-file download.

steps.title: Transport Layer
steps.description: TCP connection lifecycle: handshake, frame send/receive, zlib decompression, reconnect/backoff, best-host selection, async transport. The v1.20.0 auto-ping failover lives here — tracks failed hosts and switches to a fresh server without manual intervention. Includes ex/transport.py for the alternative MAC protocol on port 7727.

steps.title: Domain Core & Public API
steps.description: The single entry point most users touch: TdxClient (sync), AsyncTdxClient (async), the unified() facade for market info / quotes / kline / tick / transaction, MyTT (60+ technical indicators in pure Python), and the DataFrame proxy. Also exposes chanlun (缠论) analysis and the high-level realtime server.

steps.title: MAC Protocol Extension
steps.description: Alternative MAC protocol on port 7727 for futures / HK / foreign markets. Has its own command base class and frame convention (10-byte header with head_flag=0x1c). 22+ commands under src/easy_tdx/mac/commands/. Includes qfq adjustment, tick charts, capital flow, board info, auction, and ex_* commands for HK stocks.

steps.title: Backtest Engine
steps.description: Backtesting framework driven by `easy-tdx backtest`. Strategy ABC, OrderSimulator, PerformanceAnalyzer, slippage models (fixed/percentage/square-root/volume), execution models (immediate/TWAP/VWAP/limit), Brinson + factor + transaction-cost attribution. The 18 built-in strategies under strategies/*.py are thin Strategy subclasses built on MyTT.

steps.title: FastAPI Web Server
steps.description: FastAPI application exposing the library as REST endpoints. app.py handles bootstrap; the routers/ subdirectory contains 20+ feature routers (bars, block, board_mac, chanlun, finance, ex_market, etc.) backed by service modules. Designed for `easy-tdx web` and `pip install easy-tdx[web]` consumers.

steps.title: Vue 3 Frontend
steps.description: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite web UI. Pinia stores, vue-router, SFC components (charts via ECharts: KlineChart, EquityChart, CompareChart; tables: MetricTable, CompareTable; badges: GradeBadge; heatmaps: OptimizeHeatmap), and view pages (BacktestView, OptimizeView, CompareView, plus grading/mac views). Built into web-ui/dist/ which the hatchling force-include ships into the wheel — frontend must be built before pip install.

steps.title: Built-in Trading Strategies
steps.description: 18 canonical strategies users copy as templates. Categories: trend-following (ma_cross, expma_cross, macd_cross, trix_cross, dmi_trend, turtle_breakout), reversal/oscillator (rsi_reversal, kdj_golden, bias_reversal, bollinger_breakout, cci_breakout), volume-based (mfi_volume, obv_trend, volume_price), momentum (mtm_momentum), and multi-timeframe (zhuoyao_momentum). Each is a Strategy subclass using MyTT indicators.

steps.title: CI/CD & Release
steps.description: Three GitHub Actions workflows: ci.yml (matrix test on Ubuntu+Windows × Python 3.10/3.12/3.13 + mypy + frontend build), publish.yml (PyPI trusted publishing + sigstore via OIDC), release.yml (PyInstaller Windows EXE → GitHub Release). Plus readthedocs.yaml, easy_tdx.spec (PyInstaller spec), and pyproject.toml (hatchling backend with web-ui/dist force-include).
